activepieces.name: activepieces

pros: Usage-based pricing (pay per active flow) rather than a flat per-seat fee
Deployment flexibility — self-hosted or cloud
cons: Usage-based pricing per active flow can be harder to predict at scale than a flat per-seat fee

userzoom.name: UserZoom
userzoom.short_description: Enterprise UX research platform for usability testing, card sorting, tree testing, surveys, and participant recruitment at scale.

features.description: Run moderated and unmoderated usability studies on websites, apps, and prototypes with video capture and synced transcripts.
features.title: Usability Testing

features.description: Test how users group and label content to inform information architecture and navigation decisions.
features.title: Card Sorting

features.description: Validate navigation structures and menu labeling before committing to a full redesign.
features.title: Tree Testing

features.description: Measure where users click first on a design or page to evaluate visual hierarchy and clarity.
features.title: Click Testing

features.description: Field quantitative and qualitative surveys to gather attitudinal data alongside behavioral study results.
features.title: Surveys

features.description: Access a global participant network with more than 200 demographic filters, plus live-intercept recruiting for existing customers.
features.title: Participant Recruitment Panel

features.description: Track standardized usability metrics over time to measure design improvements across releases.
features.title: Usability Benchmarking

features.description: Generate automatic transcripts, timestamped notes, charts, and shareable video highlight reels for stakeholders.
features.title: Automated Analysis and Highlight Reels

features.description: Connect with tools such as Figma, Adobe Analytics, and Google Analytics to bring research into existing design and analytics workflows.
features.title: Integrations

features.description: SOC 2 Type II certification along with GDPR and HIPAA compliance support procurement in regulated industries.
features.title: Enterprise Security and Compliance

pros: Wide range of research methods (usability testing, card sorting, tree testing, click testing, surveys) in a single platform
Large built-in participant panel with detailed demographic filtering for fast recruitment
Strong reporting and highlight-reel tools for communicating findings to non-researcher stakeholders
Enterprise-grade security and compliance credentials suited to regulated industries
Supports both moderated live sessions and unmoderated self-guided studies
cons: No published self-serve pricing; requires a sales conversation and custom quote
No free trial or free plan available
Steeper learning curve than single-purpose research tools, especially for advanced study types
Product overlap and organizational transition following the 2023 UserTesting merger can create confusion for buyers
Enterprise-oriented pricing likely puts it out of reach for individual researchers and very small teams

faqs.answer: UserZoom merged with UserTesting in 2023 under common ownership. The combined company operates under the UserTesting name, and UserZoom is now marketed as a distinct product line, "UserZoom by UserTesting," within that portfolio.
faqs.question: Is UserZoom the same company as UserTesting?

faqs.answer: UserZoom does not publicly advertise a free trial or free plan. Access typically requires contacting sales for a demo and a custom quote.
faqs.question: Does UserZoom offer a free trial?

faqs.answer: UserZoom supports moderated and unmoderated usability testing, card sorting, tree testing, click testing, surveys, and usability benchmarking, along with participant recruitment through its own panel.
faqs.question: What research methods does UserZoom support?

faqs.answer: UserZoom uses a custom quote model rather than published pricing; exact cost depends on seats, study volume, and panel usage, and is only available by contacting sales.
faqs.question: How much does UserZoom cost?

faqs.answer: UserZoom is primarily used by dedicated UX research and research operations teams at mid-size to large organizations, along with the product managers, designers, and marketers who consume their findings.
faqs.question: Who typically uses UserZoom?

features: Usability testing for individuals and small teams
Global participant panel with 200+ demographic filters
Enterprise security and compliance (SOC 2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA)

google-forms.name: Google Forms
google-forms.short_description: Google Forms is Google's free tool for building online forms, surveys, and quizzes, with responses collected automatically into Google Sheets.

features.description: Multiple choice, checkboxes, dropdowns, short answer, paragraph text, linear scale, grids, date/time, and file upload questions cover most common form needs.
features.title: Multiple question types

features.description: Any form can be turned into a graded quiz with point values, answer keys, and automatic scoring and feedback.
features.title: Quiz mode with auto-grading

features.description: Basic controls for color, font, and header images let creators adjust the look of a form without design skills.
features.title: Themes and light customization

features.description: Submissions can flow directly into a linked Google Sheet or be viewed in a built-in summary with response charts.
features.title: Automatic response collection

features.description: Multiple people can build and edit the same form simultaneously, similar to how Google Docs handles shared editing.
features.title: Real-time collaborative editing

features.description: Forms can be split into sections with skip logic that routes respondents to different questions based on prior answers.
features.title: Sections and branching logic

features.description: Field-level validation rules (such as number ranges or text length) help ensure submitted data is in the expected format.
features.title: Response validation

features.description: Google Workspace Marketplace add-ons and Apps Script allow custom automation like notification emails or third-party integrations.
features.title: Add-ons and Apps Script extensibility

features.description: Pre-built templates for RSVPs, feedback surveys, quizzes, and event registration speed up form creation.
features.title: Template gallery

features.description: Forms can be shared via direct link, email invitation, embedded iframe, or social sharing buttons.
features.title: Flexible sharing options

pros: Completely free to use with no feature paywall for core form and survey functionality
Very easy to learn, with almost no setup or onboarding required
Tight integration with Google Sheets, Drive, and Google Classroom
Real-time collaborative editing lets multiple people build a form together
Works reliably on mobile devices and only requires a Google account to use
cons: Limited design and branding customization compared to dedicated survey tools
No native payment collection for orders or ticket sales
Basic analytics and reporting compared to paid survey platforms
Ties form creation and data storage to a Google account and the Google ecosystem
Conditional logic is limited to simple section-based branching, not complex multi-path flows

faqs.answer: Yes. Google Forms is free for anyone with a Google account, with no paid tier required to access its core form, survey, and quiz features.
faqs.question: Is Google Forms free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes. Forms has a built-in quiz mode that supports point values, answer keys, and automatic grading, commonly used by teachers for assessments.
faqs.question: Can I create quizzes in Google Forms?

faqs.answer: Responses can be viewed in a built-in summary tab with charts, or exported automatically to a linked Google Sheet for further analysis.
faqs.question: Where do Google Forms responses go?

faqs.answer: Not natively. Google Forms does not include built-in payment processing, so orders or paid registrations typically require a separate payment tool or add-on.
faqs.question: Can Google Forms collect payments?

faqs.answer: Form creators need an internet connection to build and edit forms, and respondents generally need a connection to submit answers, since there is no dedicated offline collection app.
faqs.question: Does Google Forms work offline?

features: Google Forms included as part of Google Workspace
Business email and additional Drive storage

qualtrics.name: Qualtrics

qualtrics.starting_price: Free plan available; paid Strategic Research plans and enterprise CX/EX suites are custom-quoted
qualtrics.headquarters: Provo, Utah / Seattle, Washington, United States

features.description: Advanced survey creation with complex branching logic, quotas, randomization, and support for many question types.
features.title: CoreXM Survey Builder

features.description: Centralized contact and panel management for organizing respondent lists, segments, and communication preferences.
features.title: XM Directory

features.description: AI-assisted text and sentiment analysis that automatically categorizes open-text feedback and surfaces themes.
features.title: Text iQ / AI Analytics

features.description: Role-based dashboards for tracking KPIs such as NPS and eNPS and sharing results across the organization.
features.title: Dashboards and Reporting

features.description: Purpose-built modules for journey mapping, case management, and frontline customer experience programs.
features.title: CX Solutions

features.description: Employee lifecycle and engagement survey programs covering onboarding, pulse surveys, and exit feedback.
features.title: EX Solutions

features.description: Prebuilt connections to CRM, helpdesk, and collaboration tools such as Salesforce and Slack.
features.title: Enterprise Integrations

features.description: Survey translation and delivery across a wide range of languages for global research programs.

features.description: A mobile app that supports offline data collection for field research and in-person surveys.

features.description: A large library of prebuilt survey and project templates to speed up study design.

pros: Powerful, enterprise-grade research and survey logic capabilities
Extensive customization, branching, and quota/randomization options
AI-assisted text and sentiment analytics reduce manual coding work
Wide integration ecosystem with CRM, helpdesk, and collaboration tools
Robust security, compliance, and administrative controls suited to large organizations
cons: Pricing is not publicly transparent and typically requires a sales conversation
Can be expensive relative to lightweight survey tools for smaller teams
Steep learning curve given the depth of features and configuration options
Free account tier is limited in active surveys and response volume
Enterprise procurement and onboarding cycles can be slow compared to self-serve tools

faqs.answer: Yes. Qualtrics offers a limited free account with a cap on active surveys and responses, and it can be temporarily upgraded to a 30-day trial of a paid plan.
faqs.question: Does Qualtrics have a free plan?

faqs.answer: Most Qualtrics enterprise suites (CX, EX, and Strategic Research) are custom-quoted based on modules, volume, and organization size, so pricing is not published; a self-service Strategic Research plan can also be purchased online.
faqs.question: How much does Qualtrics cost?

faqs.answer: Qualtrics is used for customer experience programs, employee experience and engagement surveys, academic and market research, and general-purpose survey data collection and analysis.
faqs.question: What is Qualtrics used for?

faqs.answer: The core enterprise suites target large organizations, but Qualtrics also offers a free account and a self-service paid plan that make it usable by students, researchers, and smaller teams.
faqs.question: Is Qualtrics only for large enterprises?

faqs.answer: Yes. Qualtrics includes AI-assisted features such as Text iQ for automated theme and sentiment analysis of open-text survey responses.
faqs.question: Does Qualtrics use AI?

features: Online purchase without a sales call
Core survey logic and reporting tools
Full CX, EX, or Strategy & Research suite access
Volume-based pricing on interactions or employees

sharefile.name: ShareFile
sharefile.short_description: Secure file sharing, sync, and e-signature platform for client-facing professional services firms.
sharefile.starting_price: $18.15/user/month (Advanced plan, billed monthly; lower with annual billing)
sharefile.headquarters: Raleigh, North Carolina, USA (owned by Progress Software, headquartered in Burlington, Massachusetts)

features.description: A branded, permission-controlled portal where clients upload, download, and review files without needing an internal account.
features.title: Secure client portal

features.description: AES 256-bit encryption for files at rest and in transit, with sync across desktop and mobile devices.
features.title: Encrypted file storage and sync

features.description: Built-in e-signature collection alongside document, form, and task requests sent directly to clients.
features.title: E-signature and requests

features.description: Automated, repeatable processes for recurring client engagements such as intake, onboarding, or engagement letters.
features.title: Workflow automation

features.description: Folder- and document-level permissions that determine who can view, edit, download, or share specific content.
features.title: Granular access controls

features.description: Real-time co-editing with Microsoft Office and plugins for Outlook and Gmail for secure email-based sharing.
features.title: Office and email integrations

features.description: Pre-built templates for tax engagements and other accounting workflows on higher-tier plans.
features.title: Industry-specific templates

features.description: Controls and reporting aimed at HIPAA, FINRA, and SEC recordkeeping requirements for regulated industries.
features.title: Compliance support

features.description: Watermarking, click-trail activity tracking, and NDA enforcement for M&A, audits, and litigation document rooms.
features.title: Virtual data room tools

features.description: Native apps for syncing and accessing files outside the browser across major platforms.
features.title: Desktop and mobile apps

pros: Purpose-built client portal reduces reliance on email attachments for sensitive document exchange
Strong emphasis on encryption, permissions, and audit trails for regulated industries
Industry-specific templates and workflows for accounting, legal, and financial services
Integrates with common tools such as Outlook, Gmail, and Microsoft Office
Free trial available before committing to a paid plan
cons: No free plan, and per-user pricing rises quickly across tiers
Seat minimums on most plans make it less practical for very small teams
Higher-tier features such as workflow automation and industry templates are gated behind Premium and above
Oriented around external client exchanges rather than general internal team collaboration
Initial setup of permissions, branding, and workflows can require a learning curve

faqs.answer: ShareFile is used to securely share, store, and sync files with clients, collect e-signatures and forms, and run client-facing document workflows, primarily for accounting, legal, healthcare, and financial-services firms.
faqs.question: What is ShareFile used for?

faqs.answer: ShareFile does not offer a permanent free plan; instead it offers a free trial across its paid tiers, which start with the Advanced plan and scale up through Premium, Industry Advantage, and Virtual Data Room.
faqs.question: Does ShareFile offer a free plan?

faqs.answer: ShareFile is owned by Progress Software, which completed its acquisition of ShareFile in 2024 after the product had previously operated under Citrix.
faqs.question: Who owns ShareFile?

faqs.answer: ShareFile can work for small professional-services firms with client-facing document needs, but seat minimums and per-user pricing mean very small teams or casual file-sharing use cases may find it costlier than necessary.
faqs.question: Is ShareFile suitable for small businesses?

faqs.answer: Yes, e-signature collection is built into ShareFile's Premium tier and above, alongside related tools for document, form, and task requests.
faqs.question: Does ShareFile support e-signatures?

features: Granular user permissions and document controls

wetransfer.name: WeTransfer
wetransfer.short_description: WeTransfer lets you send large files and share photos, videos, and documents online in seconds, no account needed for recipients.

features.description: Send files without an email attachment limit; recipients download directly from a link without needing a WeTransfer account.
features.title: Large File Transfers

features.description: Add a password to a transfer so only people with the code can open and download the files.
features.title: Password Protection

features.description: Control how long a download link stays active, from a short default window on free accounts to longer custom periods on paid plans.
features.title: Custom Link Expiration

features.description: See when a transfer has been opened or downloaded so you know a delivery has been received.
features.title: Transfer Tracking

features.description: Apply your own branding to the page recipients see when downloading files, available on paid tiers.
features.title: Branded Transfer Pages

features.description: Generate a link that lets other people upload files directly to you, useful for collecting assets or submissions.

features.description: Save and organize images and inspiration into shareable visual boards through WeTransfer's Collect tool.
features.title: Collect Moodboards

features.description: Native iOS and Android apps plus a Mac menu-bar app let you send files without opening a browser.
features.title: Mobile and Mac Apps

features.description: Paid plans include cloud storage for keeping and sharing files beyond a single one-off transfer.
features.title: Cloud Storage

features.description: Files are encrypted in transit and at rest while stored on WeTransfer's servers before download.
features.title: Encrypted Transfers

pros: Very simple, no-signup interface that lets recipients download without creating an account
Free tier covers occasional large-file sending without hitting typical email attachment limits
Distinctive, artist-designed visual experience that stands out from generic file-sharing tools
Paid plans scale reasonably from solo freelancers up to small collaborating teams
Native iOS, Android, and Mac apps make sending files convenient outside the browser
cons: Free plan's transfer and volume limits are easy for regular users to exceed
Files and links expire by design, so it isn't suited to long-term storage or archiving
Public developer API has been retired, limiting automation and custom integrations
Fewer collaboration and permission controls than full cloud-storage suites like Google Drive or Dropbox
Major layoffs following the 2024 Bending Spoons acquisition raise questions about long-term support pace

faqs.answer: Yes. The free plan allows a limited number of transfers and total data within a rolling 30-day period, with no account required for recipients to download.
faqs.question: Is WeTransfer free to use?

faqs.answer: On the free plan, links typically stay active for a few days; paid plans allow custom expiration windows that can be extended further.
faqs.question: How long do WeTransfer download links stay active?

faqs.answer: WeTransfer encrypts files in transit and at rest, and paid features like password protection and access controls add further security to transfers.
faqs.question: Is WeTransfer secure?

faqs.answer: No. Recipients simply click the link they receive by email or that you share directly, then download the files without signing up.
faqs.question: Do recipients need a WeTransfer account to download files?

faqs.answer: Following its 2024 acquisition by Bending Spoons, WeTransfer restructured its paid plans into Starter, Ultimate, and Teams tiers, replacing the older Pro and Premium naming.
faqs.question: What happened to WeTransfer's old Pro and Premium plans?

features: Up to 10 transfers or 3GB total every 30 days
Links stay active for a few days
Up to 10 transfers or 300GB total every 30 days
Unlimited transfers up to 1TB per transfer

form-io.name: Form.io
form-io.short_description: Self-hosted form and API platform that turns JSON-driven form schemas into APIs, PDFs, and data workflows.

features.description: Build multi-step forms with conditional logic through a visual, no-code builder.
features.title: Drag-and-drop form builder

features.description: Every form automatically becomes a RESTful API endpoint for submissions and data routing.

features.description: Convert forms to pixel-perfect PDFs or upload existing PDFs and turn them into dynamic, data-bound forms.

features.description: Build interactive data analytics and reports on top of submission data using MongoDB aggregation.
features.title: Embedded reporting

features.description: Capture cryptographic signatures that are bound to the submission data for legal validity.

features.description: Run isolated sub-projects from a single deployment, suited to SaaS and multi-client platforms.
features.title: Multi-tenancy

features.description: Control access with RBAC, SAML authentication, and audit logging across projects.
features.title: Role-based access control

pros: Open-source core avoids hard vendor lock-in and allows full self-hosting
No per-submission, per-API-call, or per-end-user usage fees
Automatic API generation removes the need to hand-build backend endpoints for every form
Enterprise features like SAML SSO, multi-tenancy, and audit logging are built in
Used by large organizations including Accenture, Deloitte, and PepsiCo
cons: Enterprise pricing is configuration-based, starting at $330/month per project or server environment
Advanced modules (PDF server, reporting UI, accessibility compliance) are priced as separate add-ons
Setup and self-hosting require developer resources rather than a simple SaaS sign-up

faqs.answer: Yes. Form.io has an open-source core, including the formio.js SDK and the Universal Agent Gateway, both available on GitHub.

faqs.answer: Yes, Form.io offers a self-hosted starter configuration in addition to its SaaS offering.
faqs.question: Can Form.io be self-hosted?

faqs.answer: Pricing is configuration-based rather than usage-based, charging per project and per API/PDF server environment instead of per form, submission, or API call.

faqs.answer: Form.io SaaS offers a 30-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.answer: Form.io lists enterprise and government customers including Accenture, Booz Allen Hamilton, Deloitte, LexisNexis, and the State of Ohio.

features: Unlimited forms, submissions, and API calls

woodpecker-ci.name: Woodpecker CI
woodpecker-ci.short_description: Woodpecker CI is a free, open-source CI/CD engine that runs pipeline steps in Docker containers, forked from Drone CI in 2019.

features.description: Each pipeline step runs inside a Docker container, keeping build environments isolated and reproducible.
features.title: Docker-based pipeline execution

features.description: Workflows are defined in YAML files stored in a .woodpecker/ directory, with steps, images, commands, and 'when' triggers for events like push.
features.title: YAML pipeline configuration

features.description: Plugins are ordinary Docker images (e.g. woodpeckerci/plugin-s3) referenced in a step, so deployment and notification logic can be shared across projects.
features.title: Plugin ecosystem

features.description: A project can define several workflows that depend on one another, rather than a single linear pipeline.
features.title: Multiple dependent workflows

features.description: Sensitive values are referenced with from_secret in step settings so credentials aren't exposed in the pipeline file.
features.title: Secret injection

features.description: Woodpecker runs entirely on infrastructure you control; there is no vendor-hosted cloud offering.
features.title: Self-hosted deployment

pros: Fully open source under Apache 2.0 and stated to remain free forever
Lightweight, container-native architecture keeps pipeline steps isolated
Plugins are ordinary Docker images, making the ecosystem easy to extend
Active community project with 7.5k+ GitHub stars
cons: No official hosted/cloud offering — requires self-hosting
Some administration and forge-setup documentation pages were unavailable at the time of review
Smaller plugin catalog than larger commercial CI platforms

faqs.answer: Yes. Woodpecker is fully open source under the Apache 2.0 license and the project states it will remain free forever.
faqs.question: Is Woodpecker CI free to use?

faqs.answer: Yes. Woodpecker was forked from Drone 0.8 in April 2019 after Drone's license changed from Apache 2.0 to a proprietary license, and was renamed Woodpecker in August 2019.
faqs.question: Is Woodpecker CI related to Drone CI?

faqs.answer: No official hosted cloud service is documented; Woodpecker is designed to be self-hosted.
faqs.question: Does Woodpecker offer a hosted cloud version?

faqs.answer: Pipelines are defined as YAML files in a .woodpecker/ directory, with steps that specify a Docker image and commands to run.
faqs.question: What format are Woodpecker pipelines written in?

faqs.answer: Enterprise IT plans start from $105/month for the Starter plan (5-15 nodes).
faqs.question: How much does Portainer cost beyond the free tier?

faqs.answer: Yes, Portainer manages both Docker and Kubernetes environments.
faqs.question: Does Portainer support Kubernetes and Docker?

faqs.answer: Yes, a Home & Student plan is available for $155/year for non-commercial use on up to 15 nodes.
faqs.question: Is there a plan for personal or student use?

opennebula.name: OpenNebula
opennebula.short_description: OpenNebula is an open-source cloud and virtualization management platform for building private, hybrid, and edge clouds as an alternative to VMware.

features.description: Automated resource scheduling and high availability for hosted workloads.
features.title: DRS-style scheduling and HA

features.description: Federate multiple sites into a single managed cloud.
features.title: Multi-site federation

features.description: Orchestrate Kubernetes clusters alongside virtualized workloads.
features.title: Kubernetes integration

features.description: Supports GPU workloads through an NVIDIA partnership.
features.title: GPU and accelerated computing

features.description: Connects with NetApp, Pure Storage, and Veeam.
features.title: Enterprise storage integrations

features.description: Isolates tenants for shared infrastructure environments.
features.title: Secure multi-tenancy

pros: Source code publicly available on GitHub
Avoids proprietary hypervisor licensing costs (positioned as a VMware alternative)
Supports GPU and multi-hypervisor environments
Multi-site federation for distributed deployments
Optional integrated Ubuntu Pro, RHEL, or SUSE subscriptions
cons: Enterprise Subscription pricing is not published on the site (quote-based)
Site content is licensed CC BY-NC-SA (non-commercial), separate from the platform's own source license
Requires infrastructure expertise to self-host and operate

faqs.answer: A Community version is available at no cost; paid Enterprise Subscriptions with additional services also exist.
faqs.question: Is OpenNebula free?

faqs.answer: It is positioned as an alternative to proprietary cloud/virtualization platforms like VMware.
faqs.question: What does OpenNebula replace?

faqs.answer: Yes, including support enabled through an NVIDIA partnership.
faqs.question: Does OpenNebula support GPU workloads?

faqs.answer: OpenNebula Systems owns and maintains the platform.
faqs.question: Who maintains OpenNebula?

limesurvey.name: LimeSurvey
limesurvey.short_description: LimeSurvey is a survey and form-building platform available as an open-source community edition or paid cloud plans.

features.description: Pre-built templates across industries speed up survey creation.
features.title: 800+ survey templates

features.description: Offers a wide range of customizable question types for building surveys and forms.
features.title: 30+ question types

features.description: Supports conditional logic to personalize the survey flow per respondent.
features.title: Logic branching and personalization

features.description: Surveys can be created and taken in over 80 languages.
features.title: Multi-language support

features.description: Built with GDPR compliance and SSL encryption for data protection.
features.title: GDPR compliance and SSL encryption

features.description: Provides response analytics and dashboards within the platform.
features.title: Built-in analytics and dashboards

features.description: Includes a beta feature for AI-assisted survey translation.
features.title: AI-assisted translations (beta)

features.description: Built to WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility standards.
features.title: Accessibility compliance

pros: Open-source Community Edition available under GPL license
Free tier lets users get started before committing to a paid plan
Broad language support (80+ languages) and 800+ templates
Serves a large user base, cited as 60K+ companies in 160+ countries
WCAG 2.2 Level AA accessibility compliance
cons: Cloud plan pricing scales with response volume and admin users, which can add up for larger surveys
Corporate plan requires contacting sales for custom pricing
Self-hosting the community edition requires managing your own server infrastructure

faqs.answer: LimeSurvey offers a free, GPL-licensed open-source Community Edition you can self-host, plus a free tier to evaluate the cloud service; paid cloud plans start at €29/month.
faqs.question: Is LimeSurvey free?

faqs.answer: LimeSurvey is headquartered in Hamburg, Germany, and was founded in 2006.
faqs.question: Where is LimeSurvey based?

faqs.answer: LimeSurvey supports more than 80 languages.
faqs.question: How many languages does LimeSurvey support?

faqs.answer: Yes, LimeSurvey is built with GDPR compliance and SSL encryption.
faqs.question: Is LimeSurvey GDPR compliant?

faqs.answer: Yes, students, teachers, and charitable organizations receive discounts of 30-50% off cloud plans.
faqs.question: Does LimeSurvey offer discounts?
